stem: Machine P produces bolts at a rate of 120 bolts per hour, and Machine Q produces bolts at a rate of 80 bolts per hour. An order requires 1,400 bolts. Both machines start simultaneously. Machine Q then breaks down when exactly $\frac{3}{7}$ of the order has been completed, and Machine P finishes the order alone. How many minutes did Machine P run alone to complete the order?
explanation:
Step 1: Find how many bolts are completed when Machine Q breaks down. $\frac{3}{7}$ of the order: $\frac{3}{7} \times 1400 = 600$ bolts completed.

Step 2: Find the remaining bolts for Machine P alone. $1400 - 600 = 800$ bolts remaining.

Step 3: Convert Machine P's rate to bolts per minute. $120 \text{ bolts/hr} = \frac{120}{60} = 2 \text{ bolts/min}$.

Step 4: Find the time Machine P runs alone. $\frac{800 \text{ bolts}}{2 \text{ bolts/min}} = 400$ minutes.

The answer is 400 minutes.

distractorRationale.A: Error type: Rate applied to wrong quantity. After Machine Q breaks down, $\frac{4}{7}$ of the order remains: $\frac{4}{7} \times 1400 = 800$ bolts. However, this student applies the combined rate (Machine P + Machine Q = 120 + 80 = 200 bolts/hr = $\frac{200}{60}$ bolts/min) to the remaining work instead of using only Machine P's rate. Time $= 800 \div \frac{200}{60} = 800 \times \frac{60}{200} = 800 \times 0.3 = 240$ minutes.
distractorRationale.B: Error type: Inverted ratio. The student misreads $\frac{3}{7}$ completed as meaning $\frac{3}{7}$ remains (i.e., confuses the completed fraction with the remaining fraction). Remaining bolts $= \frac{3}{7} \times 1400 = 600$ bolts. Machine P's rate $= 2$ bolts/min. Time $= \frac{600}{2} = 300$ minutes.
distractorRationale.D: Error type: Wrong term selected from ratio. The student correctly finds 800 bolts remaining but uses Machine Q's rate (80 bolts/hr $= \frac{80}{60}$ bolts/min) instead of Machine P's rate (120 bolts/hr $= 2$ bolts/min) for the solo phase. Time $= 800 \div \frac{80}{60} = 800 \times \frac{60}{80} = 800 \times 0.75 = 600$ minutes.
